pillars.name: Signal-Based Outbound
pillars.description: I build outbound systems that book meetings. Clay + Make + Lemlist — wired together so your pipeline doesn't depend on cold calling.
purposes: Show founders how signal-based outbound actually works
Break down the infrastructure behind booked meetings
Position the 1:1 GTM build as the obvious next step

pillars.name: AI-Native GTM Engineering
pillars.description: I'm building GTM tools with Claude Code. Replacing bloated stacks with custom-built systems that do more, cost less, and actually work.
purposes: Show what's possible when you stop duct-taping tools together
Challenge the Clay + Apollo + n8n orthodoxy
Attract technical founders who want a better way

pillars.name: Founder in the Trenches
pillars.description: I went from building outbound for Europe's biggest scaleup to doing it for SaaS founders in the Middle East. This is what the journey looks like.
purposes: Build trust through real stories and lessons
Connect with SaaS founders who are in the same fight
Make the brand human — not just a service page

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: The first outbound system I built booked 0 meetings in week one. Here's what I changed in week two.
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: I tried replacing my entire Clay workflow with Claude Code. Some things broke. Some things got 10x better.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: I moved from working with a European scaleup to serving Middle East founders. Biggest lesson: the playbook doesn't copy-paste.

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: A founder told me 'outbound doesn't work for us.' 4 weeks later he had 23 booked meetings. Here's what happened.
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: I spent a weekend building a custom prospecting tool with Claude Code. It replaced 3 paid subscriptions.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: I once sat in a pitch meeting where the founder's entire pipeline was 'referrals and hope.' That conversation changed how I sell.

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: 3 signals that tell you a prospect is ready to buy — before you ever send the first email.
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: How to use Claude Code to auto-enrich leads without paying Apollo $500/mo.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: You're a SaaS founder doing $1M ARR. Here's where your first outbound hire should actually sit in the org.

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: Client went from 0 outbound to 41 qualified meetings in 30 days. Here's the exact system we built.
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: Built a custom intent-scoring tool with Claude Code in 3 hours. It outperformed our old Clay flow.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: Just wrapped a GTM build with a Dubai-based SaaS founder. He called it 'the best money I've spent this year.'

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: Cold calling vs. signal-based outbound. One is a slot machine. The other is a system. Here's the difference.
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: What is GTM engineering? Most founders confuse it with sales ops. It's not even close.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: The 3 stages of outbound maturity for SaaS startups — and how to know which one you're stuck in.

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: I built a client's outbound system with zero warm-up. The domain got burned in 5 days. Don't do this.
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: I automated a workflow that should've stayed manual. Cost the client 200 bad leads. Here's why context matters more than speed.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: Biggest mistake I made early on: building systems before understanding the founder's ICP. Infrastructure without direction is just expensive noise.

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: How we built a full Clay + Make + Lemlist pipeline for a $2M ARR SaaS in Riyadh — from zero.
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: Replaced a founder's $1,200/mo tool stack with a Claude Code build. Same output. 80% cheaper.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: A founder came to me after burning $40k on an SDR team with no infrastructure. Here's how we rebuilt everything in 4 weeks.

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: Your competitors are still cold calling lists from 2022. Signal-based outbound is how you show up first.
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: The founders building their own GTM tools with AI will outpace everyone still duct-taping SaaS products together.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: Being in the Middle East isn't a disadvantage for outbound. It's actually an edge — and most founders don't see it yet.

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: Cold calling isn't dead. It's just the laziest way to fill a pipeline in 2025.
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: Clay is great. But if you can't build custom tools on top of it, you'll always be stuck with someone else's workflow.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: Most SaaS founders don't have a lead gen problem. They have an infrastructure problem.

ideas.Signal-Based Outbound: The exact 4-week timeline I use to take a SaaS founder from 'no outbound' to 'booked meetings every week.'
ideas.AI-Native GTM Engineering: How to evaluate whether to buy a tool or build one with Claude Code. The decision tree I use every time.
ideas.Founder in the Trenches: You're at $1M ARR and want to hit $3M. Here's where outbound fits in your growth strategy — and where it doesn't.

PS. If you're a SaaS founder doing $500k–$5M and your pipeline is inconsistent — I fix that. 4-week GTM build. DM me.
PS. I've built outbound infrastructure for 3+ startups and scaleups. Now I do it 1:1 with founders. DM 'BUILD' to talk.
